JavaScript tests for `Math.floor` behavior with various inputs including `NaN`, zero, infinity, and specific values.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.09-001.js](15.08.02.09-001.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.floor` returns `NaN` when given `NaN` as input.
- **[15.08.02.09-002.js](15.08.02.09-002.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.floor` with positive zero in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.09-003.js](15.08.02.09-003.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.floor` with negative zero in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.09-004.js](15.08.02.09-004.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.floor with Infinity in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.09-005.js](15.08.02.09-005.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.floor with negative infinity in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.09-006.js](15.08.02.09-006.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.floor` with a specific input to ensure it returns `Infinity`.
- **[15.08.02.09-007.js](15.08.02.09-007.js.md)**: Tests the equivalence of `Math.floor` and `-Math.ceil` for the value 1.2.
- **[15.08.02.09-008.js](15.08.02.09-008.js.md)**: Tests the equivalence of `Math.floor(1.9)` and `-Math.ceil(-1.9)` in JavaScript.
